title: ChatGPT prompt is working well with trading
author: bakedsmurf
selftext:
Here’s a version you could post on Reddit, written like it’s straight from you:


---

Title: How I use ChatGPT for Stocks & Options (and why it’s a game changer)

So here’s how I’ve been using ChatGPT (GPT-5) as my trading sidekick — not financial advice, just sharing what works for me.

I feed it my portfolio screenshots (Robinhood + IRA) and tell it to analyze my current positions using live data, Reddit/WSB sentiment, dark pool activity, insider trades, and technical indicators like RSI, MACD, and volume spikes. It breaks everything down cleanly — tells me what’s overbought, what’s coiling up for a move, and gives me sniper-style buy/sell setups with stop-loss and trailing-stop levels.

For options, I’ll ask it stuff like:

> “Is this $RIOT $7 call a good buy right now?”
or
“Would you take a LEAP on this setup with $1k risk?”



It’ll run through strike selection, IV crush, expiration timing, break-even math, and even give me probabilities like “70% chance this setup pays if BTC momentum continues.”

I basically use it as a real-time trading coach that checks charts, sentiment, and fundamentals faster than I ever could. Then I decide the moves myself.

It’s not magic. It just works. 

If you actually use it right (feed it data, ask it to use live market info, Reddit chatter, etc.), it’s like having a calm, insanely fast trading partner who doesn’t sleep or get FOMO.

replies.author: bakedsmurf
replies.body: Im risking 5-7% some up to 10% usually with trailing stops all given by AI.  Hard stops are sometimes also used when its on a lower side of its consolidation phase. There is a strict criteria for buying. There's been losses but controlled. The patterns it finds are not every day but when they've hit. Its been a good few months doing this.  Like it had me put 40% of my portfolio into BITF stock and options as it consolidated. it did have me sell in tiers that I could have 3x but still it was fun selling those. But im still my last 2 LEAP calls its having me hold peaked at 700% this morning. These were like .22 when I got in. I wouldn't say im an expert, but I've lost a shit ton without AI while im doubling my robinhood, and almost tripling my IRA in 2 years.

replies.author: bakedsmurf
replies.body: It will tell me to hold cash for periods. It'll also tell me not to chase tops and stay away from down trends. "No sniper setups standby" when nothings hitting.  Not every trade has hit, but when they do, they usually double at least, and it tells me where to keep a stop loss.  So im never risking more than 7 -10 % on a trade. Like today it had me buy a LEAP call but also told me where to set alerts for a reversal to get out.

replies.author: Doma96
replies.body: Can you send me your exact prompt 🙏🏻

replies.author: bakedsmurf
replies.body:
> You are my personal stock market genius.

I have two accounts:
🧨 Robinhood = YOLO plays — high-risk, high-reward swings and short-term momentum trades based on volume, sentiment, catalysts, and Reddit/WSB chatter.
🧠 Schwab IRA = Long-term compounders — only quality growth monsters, no hype, no trash.

Your job: Make me money using live data, technicals, sentiment, and catalysts.
Every trade setup must include:

1. Ticker


2. Account (Robinhood or Schwab IRA)


3. Entry price/range (live)


4. Stop loss


5. Target price or long-term goal


6. Conviction level (1–9)


7. What makes it sexy — breakout, earnings beat, trend reversal, insider activity, Reddit surge, etc.



Use data from: live markets, Reddit/WSB sentiment, dark pool prints, insider buys, and trading volume spikes.

Rules:

Always timestamp your calls.

Use screenshots of my portfolios when I send them — analyze them for current prices, gains, and positioning.

Simplify instructions: clear buy/sell/hold with targets, no fluff.

Robinhood = momentum, speed, and short-term wins.

 IRA = sniper-style long entries, smart trailing stops, and compounding growth.

If there’s no clean setup, say “stand down” instead of forcing a trade.


Style: Talk like a trader who’s hungry to win but disciplined like a sniper.
Reference big market movers, Fed decisions, or major catalysts when needed.
If I say “use live data,” scan all sources and base your answer on current info.

When I send screenshots — read them like a trading terminal. Identify winners, losers, open positions, and next moves.

No education fluff. No “depends on your goals.” Just sniper setups that hit hard.
